name: SAPWatch
tagline: Real-time Log Intelligence for SAP Landscapes
problem: SAP Basis teams spend hours manually reviewing massive syslog, workprocess, and ICM log files across multiple SAP instances. Critical errors get buried in noise, and incidents are detected only after users complain. There is no centralized, real-time view of log health across the SAP landscape.
solution: SAPWatch streams SAP system logs via RFC/OData to AWS, where CloudWatch Logs and Lambda-powered pattern recognition detect anomalies in real time. Machine learning models trained on historical SAP log patterns identify emerging issues before they escalate. Alerts are delivered via SNS with contextual SAP transaction codes and recommended resolution steps.
riskNote: Requires RFC connectivity to customer SAP systems; log volume can be very high during peak periods. Need to handle SAP-specific encoding and multiline log formats.
model: SaaS subscription per SAP SID monitored

name: BasisPulse
problem: SAP customers lack a single-pane-of-glass view that quantifies the overall health of their SAP landscape. Basis administrators juggle multiple SAP transactions (SM21, ST22, SM37, SM66) and cannot easily communicate system status to management in business terms.
solution: BasisPulse connects to SAP systems via RFC and aggregates metrics from key monitoring transactions into a unified health score. AWS Lambda functions poll SAP at configurable intervals, normalize data into a composite score (0-100), and store time-series data in DynamoDB. A React dashboard visualizes trends, and SNS notifications fire when scores drop below thresholds.
riskNote: Well-understood SAP monitoring interfaces. Main challenge is tuning the health score algorithm to reflect real-world system conditions across different SAP module configurations.
model: SaaS subscription per SAP landscape

name: SAPTrace
problem: SAP HANA performance issues are difficult to diagnose. Expensive SQL statements, memory-intensive column store operations, and delta merge problems silently degrade system performance. DBAs lack historical trend data and automated root cause analysis for HANA-specific bottlenecks.
solution: SAPTrace connects to HANA monitoring views (M_SQL_PLAN_CACHE, M_EXPENSIVE_STATEMENTS, M_CS_ALL_COLUMNS) and streams performance telemetry to AWS. Lambda functions analyze query execution plans, detect regression patterns, and correlate performance anomalies with system events. Results are stored in S3 as Parquet files for long-term trend analysis via Athena.
riskNote: HANA monitoring views vary between versions (1.0 vs 2.0). Requires read access to HANA system schema. Large HANA systems can generate significant telemetry volume.
model: SaaS subscription per HANA instance

name: SAPUptime
problem: SAP customers with SLA commitments to internal business units or external clients cannot easily calculate and report actual uptime and availability metrics. Manual tracking in spreadsheets is error-prone and does not account for planned maintenance windows, partial outages, or degraded performance states.
solution: SAPUptime continuously monitors SAP system availability via CCMS alerts and synthetic transaction checks. Lambda functions process availability events, calculate SLA metrics excluding planned maintenance windows, and generate automated monthly SLA reports. Data is stored in DynamoDB with S3 for long-term retention, and reports are delivered via SES.
riskNote: SLA calculation logic must be precise and auditable. Planned maintenance window management requires clear UX. Different customers define 'availability' differently.
model: SaaS subscription per SAP landscape

name: SoDMatrix
problem: Defining and maintaining SoD (Segregation of Duties) rulesets for SAP GRC is extremely complex. Companies spend months building SoD matrices from scratch, and existing rules become outdated as SAP roles and transaction codes evolve. Auditors frequently find gaps in SoD coverage.
solution: SoDMatrix provides a cloud-based rule builder with pre-built SoD templates based on industry best practices (SOX, GDPR, FDA). Users can customize conflict definitions, simulate rule changes against exported SAP role data, and export finalized matrices for import into SAP GRC. AI-powered suggestions identify commonly missed conflict scenarios.
riskNote: SoD rules vary significantly by industry and regulatory framework. Requires deep SAP authorization expertise to validate rule quality. Compliance-sensitive data handling requirements.
model: SaaS subscription with tiered pricing by rule complexity

name: SAPLicense Pro
problem: SAP licensing audits are a major financial risk for enterprises. Indirect access through third-party integrations, RPA bots, and custom interfaces creates hidden license exposure. Companies overpay by 20-40% because they lack visibility into actual license consumption patterns.
solution: SAPLicense Pro analyzes SAP user activity logs, RFC call records, and interface traffic to build a complete picture of license consumption including indirect access. Lambda functions process SAP statistical records (ST03N data), classify usage patterns, and recommend optimal license type assignments. Interactive dashboards show potential savings.
riskNote: SAP licensing terms are complex and frequently change. Recommendations must be legally accurate. Customers may fear triggering audit attention by analyzing their own usage.
model: SaaS subscription plus success-based fee on identified savings

name: SAPAudit Trail
problem: SAP's native audit logging (SM20, Security Audit Log) can be tampered with by privileged users. Regulatory requirements (SOX, GDPR, GxP) increasingly demand immutable, tamper-proof audit trails that exist outside the SAP system being audited.
solution: SAPAudit Trail captures SAP security events in real time and stores them in an immutable, cryptographically verifiable ledger on AWS. Events are streamed via RFC to Lambda, written to S3 with Object Lock (WORM compliance), and indexed in DynamoDB for fast querying. Each entry is hash-chained to the previous, creating a verifiable audit chain.
riskNote: Must guarantee zero data loss for compliance. SAP Security Audit Log configuration varies between systems. S3 Object Lock governance vs compliance mode decision has cost implications.
model: SaaS subscription based on event volume

name: SAPVuln
problem: SAP systems are targeted by sophisticated attackers, but most vulnerability scanners do not understand SAP-specific attack vectors. Missing SAP Security Notes, insecure RFC configurations, open ICF services, and default credentials create an expanding attack surface that generic tools miss.
solution: SAPVuln performs automated security assessments against SAP systems using SAP-specific vulnerability checks. Lambda functions execute a library of 200+ security checks covering RFC gateway security, ICF service exposure, user authorization analysis, and missing Security Notes. Results are scored using CVSS and prioritized by exploitability.
riskNote: Security scanning could trigger IDS alerts or affect system performance if not throttled. Vulnerability database must be continuously updated. Customers need strong assurance about data security.
model: SaaS subscription per SAP system scanned

name: SAPLoad
problem: Traditional SAP performance testing tools are expensive, require dedicated infrastructure, and cannot simulate realistic peak loads. Companies migrating to S/4HANA or scaling for seasonal peaks have no cost-effective way to validate system capacity before go-live.
solution: SAPLoad generates realistic SAP workloads using distributed Lambda functions that simulate concurrent SAP GUI, RFC, and OData users. Test scenarios are defined using recorded SAP transaction flows. AWS auto-scaling ensures tests can ramp to thousands of virtual users without dedicated infrastructure. Results are analyzed in real time with CloudWatch dashboards.
riskNote: Generating realistic SAP dialog load requires deep protocol knowledge. Tests against production systems need careful safeguards. SAP GUI protocol simulation is technically complex.
model: Pay-per-test with subscription for recurring testing

name: SAPBaseline
problem: After SAP transports, patches, or configuration changes, subtle performance regressions go undetected until users complain weeks later. There is no automated way to compare pre-change and post-change performance baselines for critical SAP business processes.
solution: SAPBaseline captures SAP response time metrics for key transactions and dialog steps, establishing statistical baselines. After each change event (transport, patch, config), automated comparison runs detect statistically significant regressions. Lambda functions analyze ST03N workload data and STAD transaction-level records to pinpoint degradation sources.
riskNote: Statistical baseline models need sufficient historical data to avoid false positives. SAP workload patterns vary by day of week and business cycle. Must distinguish real regression from normal variation.
model: SaaS subscription per SAP production system

name: SAPPatch
problem: Applying SAP Security Notes and Support Packages is a time-consuming, error-prone manual process. Basis teams must evaluate dozens of notes per month, check prerequisites, resolve conflicts, and coordinate maintenance windows. Many customers fall months behind on critical security patches.
solution: SAPPatch automates the SAP patching lifecycle from note evaluation to deployment validation. Lambda functions pull the latest SAP Security Notes via SAP's API, cross-reference with installed components, and generate prioritized patching plans. Step Functions orchestrate multi-system deployment sequences with automated rollback on failure.
riskNote: Automated patching of production SAP systems carries inherent risk. Rollback procedures must be bulletproof. SAP Note dependency resolution is complex. Customer trust is critical.
model: SaaS subscription per managed SAP landscape

name: SAPTransport
problem: SAP transport management across multi-system landscapes (DEV > QAS > PRD) is plagued by conflicts, missing dependencies, and incorrect import sequences. Transport errors cause system downtime and are a leading cause of production incidents in SAP environments.
solution: SAPTransport analyzes transport request dependencies using graph algorithms, detects conflicts before import, and orchestrates optimal import sequences across the landscape. Lambda functions connect to SAP's Transport Management System, build dependency graphs stored in DynamoDB, and use Step Functions to coordinate sequential imports with automated pre- and post-checks.
riskNote: Transport dependency analysis must handle SAP's complex object types. Automated import into production requires robust error handling. Integration with existing ChaRM/Focused Build processes needed.
model: SaaS subscription per SAP transport domain

name: SAPUser
problem: Managing SAP user accounts across multiple systems is manual and error-prone. New hire provisioning takes days, role assignments drift from approved profiles, and terminated users retain access for weeks. This creates security risks and audit findings.
solution: SAPUser automates the full SAP user lifecycle from onboarding to offboarding. Lambda functions integrate with corporate identity providers (Azure AD, Okta) via EventBridge to trigger automated SAP user provisioning. Role assignments follow approved templates, periodic access reviews are automated, and deprovisioning occurs instantly upon termination events.
riskNote: SAP user management BAPIs have limitations for complex role assignments. Integration with each IdP requires specific connector development. Must handle SAP's composite role hierarchy correctly.
model: SaaS subscription based on managed user count

name: SAPBackup
problem: SAP teams take regular backups but rarely validate recovery procedures. When disaster strikes, teams discover corrupt backups, missing archive logs, or untested recovery procedures. HANA backup validation is particularly complex due to data and log backup interdependencies.
solution: SAPBackup automates periodic backup validation and recovery testing for SAP HANA and traditional databases. Lambda functions orchestrate automated restore tests to isolated AWS environments using ECS Fargate containers, verify data consistency, and generate compliance reports. Step Functions manage the full test lifecycle including environment provisioning and cleanup.
riskNote: HANA restore requires significant compute resources. Automated recovery testing needs isolated network environments. Backup file sizes can be very large (terabytes). Cost management for restore infrastructure is critical.
model: SaaS subscription plus compute costs for restore testing

name: SAPMigrate
problem: Companies planning to migrate SAP to AWS need to assess their current landscape complexity, identify migration blockers, estimate effort, and plan the migration sequence. Current assessments are manual consulting engagements that take weeks and cost tens of thousands of dollars.
solution: SAPMigrate performs automated cloud readiness assessments by analyzing SAP system configuration, custom code inventory, interface landscape, and data volumes. Lambda functions process uploaded system extracts (ST02, DB02, SE16N exports), score migration complexity across dimensions, and generate a prioritized migration roadmap with AWS architecture recommendations.
riskNote: Assessment quality depends on completeness of input data. Migration complexity scoring requires calibration against real-world migration outcomes. Recommendations must stay current with SAP's evolving cloud strategy.
model: Per-assessment fee with subscription for ongoing monitoring

name: SAPCode Scanner
problem: SAP customers have millions of lines of custom ABAP code that may contain security vulnerabilities, performance anti-patterns, and S/4HANA compatibility issues. SAP's built-in Code Inspector has limited rules and no centralized reporting across systems.
solution: SAPCode Scanner analyzes uploaded ABAP source code against an extensive rule library covering security (SQL injection, authorization checks), performance (SELECT *, nested loops, missing indexes), and S/4HANA readiness (deprecated APIs, simplification items). Lambda functions parse ABAP syntax, apply 500+ rules, and generate prioritized remediation reports with code fix suggestions powered by Bedrock.
riskNote: ABAP parsing is complex with many syntax variations. False positive rate must be low to maintain credibility. Rule library requires ongoing maintenance as SAP releases new versions.
model: SaaS subscription based on lines of code analyzed

name: SAPCost
tagline: AWS Cost Attribution for SAP Workloads
problem: Organizations running SAP on AWS struggle to attribute cloud costs to specific SAP modules, business units, or cost centers. AWS Cost Explorer does not understand SAP-specific resource consumption patterns, making chargeback and showback reporting impossible.
solution: SAPCost combines AWS Cost and Usage Report data with SAP workload metadata to provide granular cost attribution. Lambda functions correlate EC2/RDS costs with SAP application server roles, map storage costs to SAP data categories, and allocate shared infrastructure costs using SAP-aware splitting algorithms. Interactive dashboards in QuickSight show cost trends by SAP module, business unit, and environment.
riskNote: Cost allocation logic must be transparent and auditable. AWS pricing model changes require rule updates. Multi-account SAP landscapes add complexity to cost aggregation.
model: SaaS subscription based on monthly AWS spend managed

name: SAPInsights
problem: SAP processes like Order-to-Cash, Procure-to-Pay, and Record-to-Report contain hidden inefficiencies, bottlenecks, and compliance deviations. Traditional process mining tools require expensive licenses and complex data extraction. Most SAP customers have never analyzed their actual process execution patterns.
solution: SAPInsights extracts SAP change document and workflow log data to reconstruct actual business process flows. Lambda functions process SAP table extracts (CDHDR, BKPF, VBAK, EKKO), apply process mining algorithms, and visualize discovered process maps. AI-powered analysis via Bedrock identifies deviations from ideal process flows and quantifies improvement opportunities.
riskNote: SAP table structures vary between versions and industries. Process mining algorithms need optimization for large datasets (millions of events). Data extraction from production SAP systems must be carefully managed.
model: SaaS subscription with tiered pricing by process complexity

name: SAPCapacity
tagline: Predictive Capacity Planning for SAP on AWS
problem: SAP systems on AWS are either over-provisioned (wasting money) or under-provisioned (risking performance). Capacity planning is based on gut feeling rather than data. Seasonal peaks, month-end processing, and business growth make static sizing obsolete within months.
solution: SAPCapacity collects SAP workload metrics (dialog steps, RFC calls, background job duration, HANA memory consumption) and correlates them with AWS resource utilization. ML models predict future resource needs based on business growth patterns. Lambda functions generate right-sizing recommendations and automated scaling schedules aligned to SAP workload patterns.
riskNote: ML prediction accuracy depends on sufficient historical data. SAP workload patterns can shift abruptly due to business events. Recommendations must account for SAP-specific constraints (e.g., HANA memory cannot be auto-scaled).
model: SaaS subscription per SAP landscape managed

name: SAPBenchmark
tagline: Industry Peer Benchmarking for SAP Operations
problem: SAP customers have no way to compare their operational metrics (system performance, patch currency, security posture, cloud spend efficiency) against industry peers. Without benchmarks, teams cannot justify investment in improvements or prove the value of their SAP operations to management.
solution: SAPBenchmark aggregates anonymized operational metrics from participating SAP customers to create industry benchmarks. Lambda functions collect and anonymize metrics via a secure opt-in process, compute percentile rankings, and generate benchmark reports. Customers see where they stand vs. industry peers across key operational dimensions without exposing raw data.
riskNote: Requires critical mass of participants for meaningful benchmarks. Data anonymization must be provably secure. Customers may be reluctant to share operational data even anonymized. Industry segmentation needs careful design.
model: Freemium with premium tier for detailed benchmarks
